samples.gvd:
module synchronizer
(
    input clk_a,
    input clk_b,
    input arstn,
    input brstn,
    input [3:0] data_in,
    input data_en,
    output reg [3:0] dataout
);

    reg [3:0] data_reg;
    reg [3:0] en_data_reg;
    reg en_clap_one;
    reg en_clap_two;
    
    always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
        if(!arstn) begin
            data_reg <= 4'd0;
        end
        else begin
            data_reg <= data_in;
        end
    end
    
    always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
        if(!arstn) begin
            en_data_reg <= 4'd0;
        end
        else begin
            en_data_reg <= data_en;
        end
    end
    
    always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
        if(!brstn) begin
            en_clap_one <= 1'd0;
        end
        else begin
            en_clap_one <= en_data_reg;
        end
    end
    
    always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
        if(!brstn) begin
            en_clap_two <= 1'd0;
        end
        else begin
            en_clap_two <= en_clap_one;
        end
    end
    
    always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
        if(!brstn) begin
            dataout <= 4'd0;
        end
        else begin
            if(en_clap_two) begin
                dataout <= data_reg;
            end
            else begin
                dataout <= dataout;
            end
        end
    end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer. Clock signal A. Clock signal B. Active-low reset signal A. Active-low reset signal B. Input data signal. Input enable signal. Output data signal. Register to store input data. Register to store input enable signal. Control register for selection of input data. Control register for selection of input data.

Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer.

Register data_reg to store input data signal data_in. Update data_reg with data_in on the positive edge of clk_a or the negative edge of arstn. Reset data_reg to 0 when arstn is low.

Register en_data_reg to store input enable signal data_en. Update en_data_reg with data_en on the positive edge of clk_a or the negative edge of arstn. Reset en_data_reg to 0 when arstn is low.

Register en_clap_one and en_clap_two for controlling the selection of input data. Update en_clap_one with en_data_reg on the positive edge of clk_b or the negative edge of brstn. Reset en_clap_one to 0 when brstn is low. Update en_clap_two with en_clap_one on the positive edge of clk_b or the negative edge of brstn. Reset en_clap_two to 0 when brstn is low.

Assign output dataout based on en_clap_two. If en_clap_two is high, assign data_reg to dataout. If en_clap_two is low, assign dataout to its previous value.
